firstPetitionIntroLines: The clerk lays down the first docket. The colony watches how you breathe.
They come to test the shape of your authority. Not with swords. With requests.
The first petitions are simple. That will not last.
petitionArrivalLines: Another request comes in, and it is not really just a request.
The next problem arrives dressed up as advice.
By midday, somebody else has decided what the colony needs first.
A simpler version of this issue probably existed ten minutes ago. It does not anymore.
What they are asking for is clear. Why they are asking now is the more useful question.
optionConfirmationLines: That solves one thing and wakes up another.
A clean answer on paper. We will see about the ground.
That should hold, assuming people act like they said they would.
Reasonable enough. Which usually means the consequences will be less reasonable.
Not ideal, but at least it is a decision.
successFlavorLines: For once, the outcome mostly matched the plan.
It worked better than anyone had a right to expect.
People are still complaining, but less than before, which counts as progress.
The colony looks a little more intentional after this.
mixedFlavorLines: The main issue calmed down. The side issue did not.
This bought time, which is useful until it runs out.
Nobody got exactly what they wanted, which may be why it held together.
badFlavorLines: The problem did not disappear. It just changed shape.
That landed poorly, and people noticed.
The fast answer turned out to be the expensive one.
Now the issue is bigger and has witnesses.
